data.title: Iraq is waiting for Turkey's approval to restart oil flow from Kurdistan, minister says
data.description:
Iraq is waiting for Turkey's approval to restart the oil flow from the Iraqi Kurdistan region, the Iraqi oil minister said on Monday.

Kurdish oil exports will hopefully be ready in two days, Hayan Abdel-Ghani told reporters.

Asked about when Iraq will resume oil exports from the Kurdish region, he said "this issue will be settled in a week."

Iraqi Kurdistan authorities have agreed with the federal oil ministry to restart Kurdish crude exports based on available volumes, Kurdistan's regional government said on Sunday.

The pipeline was halted by Turkey in March 2023 after the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) ordered Ankara to pay Baghdad $1.5 billion in damages for unauthorised exports between 2014 and 2018.

U.S. President Donald Trump's administration is putting pressure on Iraq to allow Kurdish oil exports to restart or face sanctions alongside Iran, sources have told Reuters. An Iraqi official later denied pressure or the threat of sanctions.

A speedy resumption of exports from Iraq's semi-autonomous Kurdistan region would help to offset a potential fall in Iranian oil exports, which Washington has pledged to cut to zero as part of Trump's "maximum pressure" campaign against Tehran.

data.title: Update: Tesla, Stellantis, Volkswagen, BYD Under Probe by Italian Regulator Over EV Claims
data.description:
(Updates with attribution to the Italian regulator's statement throughout the story.)

Tesla , Stellantis , Volkswagen, and BYD are under investigation by Italy's antitrust agency for misleading buyers on battery capabilities of their electric vehicles, the Italian Competition Authority said Friday.

The inquiries focus on potentially unfair commercial practices regarding EV driving range, battery capacity degradation, and limitations on battery-related warranties, according to the statement.

Inspections were conducted at the Italian offices of all the automakers Thursday, with support from Italy's financial crimes police, the regulator said.

Tesla, Stellantis, Volkswagen, and BYD did not respond to MT Newswires' requests for comment.

data.title: German Businesses Confidence Remains Weak Amid Election Fallout
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Business sentiment in Germany held at a weak level this month, with companies keeping a wait-and-see approach surrounding the economic impact of Sunday's elections and potential trade tariffs from the U.S.

The Ifo Institute's business-climate index held at 85.2 in February, the same as January, albeit a little worse than expected by economists polled by The Wall Street Journal.

The relatively weak reading reflects the difficulty in turning around Germany's fortunes for incoming Chancellor Friedrich Merz, whose Christian Democratic Union scored a comfortable victory in the elections this weekend. He has said he hopes to put together a coalition before Easter in April.

"The German economy is waiting to see how things develop," Ifo President Clemens Fuest said.

While companies were slightly less satisfied with their current business, expectations brightened somewhat, he said.

Germany's economy contracted for a second straight year in 2024, with economists penciling in weak growth this year.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Deliveroo Likely to Be the Next M&A Target — Market Talk
data.description: Deliveroo is likely to be the next short-term M&A target, Bryan Garnier analyst Clement Genelot writes in a note. Prosus's deal to buy Just Eat Takeaway for $4.29 billion revived M&A activity in the European food-delivery sector, Genelot says. "DoorDash has been circling around Deliveroo since early 2024 and the acquisition of JET might generate a sentiment of emergency, prompting DoorDash to resume discussions with Deliveroo," he adds. Deliveroo and Just Eat shares are up 5.8% and 52.8%, respectively. Meanwhile, Prosus shares are trading 7% lower. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Siemens Energy, Schneider Electric fall on report Microsoft cancels lease for AI Data Centers
data.description:
** German firm Siemens Energy's and French company Schneider Electric shares fall, as analysts point to TD Cowen's note, reporting that Microsoft is cancelling leases for AI data centers

** TD Cowen notes that Microsoft has recently cancelled "a couple hundred MWs" of lease capacity, adding that it comes on the heels of checks last month indicating that the company was walking away from new DC lease capacity it had previously been interested in

** "While hard to decipher, what seems more clear is that these developments suggest Microsoft is running at over-capacity right now," TD Cowen writes

** Siemens Energy's stock falls 5.1% to the bottom of Germany's blue-chip index , while Schneider Electric falls 4.2%

data.title: BASF to Increase Production Capacity Of Cellasto in India
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

BASF will construct and operate a new plant in India to increase its production capacity of microcellular polyurethane Cellasto.

The German chemical company said Monday that the new plant is expected to be operational in the second half of 2026.

The company didn't disclose financial details.

BASF develops, manufactures and markets a wide variety of automotive components made from its specialized MCU elastomers under the Cellasto brand.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: SNIPR Biome Strengthens IP Portfolio with Patent Granted for a Novel CRISPR-Cas System
data.description:
COPENHAGEN, Denmark, Feb. 24, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — SNIPR BIOME ApS (“SNIPR” or “the Company”), a clinical stage biotech company pioneering CRISPR-based therapies, today announces grant of patent EP4392563B on February 24th 2025 by the European Patent Office protecting the newly-discovered and characterised CRISPR-CasS™ system. This system and its uses do not rely on Cas9 or Cas3. The patent covers use of the new CasS system in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, as well as when fused to effector domains conferring additional functionality, such as base editors and nuclease domains. Commercial licenses are available.

This new patent adds to the extensive worldwide patent portfolio of SNIPR Biome, including more than 60 granted patents worldwide, protecting technology platforms for editing prokaryotes and eukaryotes using CRISPR-Cas. SNIPR's patent filings were the first in the field to demonstrate the selective killing of bacteria in a mixed species population (i.e., a microbiome). This is exemplified by granted US patent US11,517,582, which covers the use of CRISPR-Cas systems to achieve selective killing of bacteria by at least 1000-fold in situations where bacteria are growing in a microbiome. This patent covers the use of any type of CRISPR-Cas and the targeting of any bacterial species. Broad protection has also been granted by the European Patent Office, and further examples of SNIPR's granted patents can be found on their website (https://www.sniprbiome.com/snipr-biome-ip)

In 2023 the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit issued a precedential ruling in favor of SNIPR in a CRISPR patent interference case with Rockefeller University, thereby upholding SNIPR’s patents.

The Company makes its patents available for academic and non-profit research use without a written license. Parties interested in commercially licensing SNIPR’s intellectual property should contact the Company at partnering@sniprbiome.com.

About

SNIPR BIOME is a Danish clinical-stage biotech company pioneering the development of precision medicines using CRISPR technology for microbial gene therapy. The company is pioneering a novel use of CRISPR-Cas technology to better treat and prevent human diseases through precision killing of bacteria or gene modification. SNIPR BIOME was the first company to orally dose humans with a CRISPR therapeutic and the first company to have been granted US and European patents for the use of CRISPR for targeting microbiomes. SNIPR technology is used in collaborations with Novo Nordisk A/S, CARB-X, the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, SPRIN-D, and MD Anderson Cancer Center. For more information, visit www.sniprbiome.com SNIPR BIOME is backed by a strong syndicate of investors including Lundbeckfonden Biocapital, EQT, North East Family Office and Wellington Partners.

Contact

SNIPR BIOME: Christian Grøndahl | +45 2020 2747 | cg@sniprbiome.com

Footer: “CasS” and “CRISPR-CasS” are trademarks of SNIPR Biome.

data.title: China Zenith Chemical to Sell Up to HK$50 Million Bonds
data.description:
China Zenith Chemical Group is planning to issue up to 50 non-convertible bonds with a principal amount of HK$1 million each and an issue amount not exceeding HK$50 million, a Feb. 21 filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The bonds will carry an interest rate of 10% per annum and will fall due on the first anniversary of the issue date.

The chemical company will use proceeds from the issue for debt repayment and general working capital.

data.title: Russian rouble up slightly vs US dollar on Ukraine peace hopes
data.description:
The Russian rouble traded at a five and a half month high against the U.S. dollar on Monday, on expectations of a peaceful settlement in Ukraine as the war enters its fourth year.

At 0920 GMT the rouble was up 0.7% at 87.90 against the dollar in the over-the-counter market, the strongest since early September. The rouble weakened 0.4% to 12.06 against China's yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia.

"The triggers for strengthening have not changed – the news background, expectations of the removal of the geopolitical premium, as well as the gradual increase in exporters' activity," said PSB bank analyst Denis Popov.

The Russian currency has gained 23% against the dollar and 12% against the yuan this year, mostly on expectations of better relations with the United States under President Donald Trump.

The rouble is still down about 10% since February 24, 2022, when Russian President Vladimir Putin sent troops into Ukraine.

"If we talk about the exchange rate of exactly 88 roubles per dollar, it is certainly stronger than the balance we have seen and which is currently in our forecasts," Russia's Economy Minister Maxim Reshetnikov was quoted by the Russian agencies as saying.

The rouble did not move on the latest EU sanctions package, which included a ban on primary aluminium imports and sales of gaming consoles, as well as listing owners and operators of 74 so-called shadow fleet vessels used to evade sanctions.

Dollar/rouble futures on MOEX, which serve as guidance for over-the-counter rates, were up 0.1% at 89.15. The Russian central bank set the official exchange rate at 88.17.

data.title: Saudi's Ades Holding Posts 2024 Earnings Beat Amid Local, International Expansion
data.description:
Ades Holding Co. (SASE:2382) said Monday its 2024 profit nearly doubled year over year, buoyed by a 43.1% surge in revenue driven by continued expansion in Saudi Arabia and fresh revenue streams from emerging markets.

For the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, Ades posted a net profit of 816.2 million riyals, a significant increase from 452.1 million riyals in the prior year. The Saudi Arabia-listed drilling services company's performance was supported by a revenue jump to 6.20 billion riyals from 4.33 billion riyals earlier.

The company attributed the revenue boost to ongoing local expansion efforts, particularly the deployment of new rigs, alongside new contributions from India, Indonesia, and Thailand. Increased onshore activity in Kuwait and Algeria further supported growth, while improved daily rates on existing offshore rigs in Saudi Arabia and Egypt added to the momentum.

Consequently, Ades' net profit margin expanded to 13.2% from 10.4%. Meanwhile, lower financing costs relative to revenue helped mitigate the impact of increased depreciation expenses incurred from rig deployments across Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, India, Algeria, and Southeast Asia.

Net profit growth also reflects a bargain purchase gain from the acquisition of two jackup rigs from Vantage Drilling International, although this was partially offset by a non-cash provision for other taxes and various non-recurring expenses.

The company's strong results prompted the board to declare a dividend of 0.22 riyal per share in the second half of 2024 after paying 0.216 riyal per share for the first half. The second interim dividend is scheduled for payment on March 24, 2025, with an eligibility date set for March 10, 2025.

Speaking on the company's outlook for 2025, Ades Chief Executive Mohamed Farouk noted that "We are confident that the underlying supply-demand dynamics in our industry will continue to favour our high-performing and highly demanded fleet of optimized assets, with strong tendering activity in markets like Southeast Asia, the Middle East and West Africa ensuring sustainable growth and profitability."

The stock was up almost 3% in afternoon trade.

data.title: Euro and German stocks climb after conservatives win election
data.description:
Key points:
  • DAX up, Euro hits one-month high as Merz wins German vote
  • Germany likely heading for centrist grand coalition
  • Wall St futures bounce ahead of Nvidia earnings

The euro and German stocks rallied on Monday as investors welcomed Germany’s election result that put centrist parties on track to form a coalition, although optimism was tempered by potentially tricky negotiations over economic policy.

European shares ticked higher while Wall Street futures also edged up after a sharp U.S. sell-off on Friday.

Friedrich Merz was set to become Germany's next chancellor after his opposition conservatives won the national election on Sunday. Merz should be able to form a so-called grand coalition with the ruling centre-left Social Democrats, even though it slumped to third behind the far-right Alternative for Germany.

"In the end (it was) a result that was close to the latest exit polls and should be a very market-friendly outcome," said Peter Schaffrik, global macro strategist at RBC Capital Markets.

The euro rose to a one-month high of $1.0528 before dipping to last trade 0.22% higher at $1.0481.

"While Merz seems determined to ease off the so-called debt brake, which limits annual borrowing to 0.35% of GDP, it won’t be straightforward, because he will need a two-third majority in parliament," said Susannah Streeter, head of money and markets, Hargreaves Lansdown.

Meanwhile Germany's DAX stock index rose 0.73% in early trading. The pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 0.19%, although tech stocks slipped.

German coalition talks start as EU leaders are set to hold an extraordinary summit on March 6 to discuss additional support for Ukraine and how to pay for European defence needs.

This week marks three years since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

WALL STREET STEADIES

S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq futures both gained 0.6%. The Nasdaq fell 2.5% last week, its worst week in three months, with losses led by the "Magnificent Seven" tech firms.

Wall Street took a hit on Friday when a survey on services showed a shock slide in activity amid concerns about tariffs and cost pressures.

That pullback raised the stakes for Nvidia's results on Wednesday where investors are looking for fourth-quarter sales around $38.5 billion and first-quarter guidance around $42.5 billion from the chip-making company.

The Federal Reserve's favoured measure of core inflation is due on Friday and expected to show a slowdown to 2.6% from 2.8%, but could be overshadowed by tariff worries.

A survey of U.S. consumers out on Friday showed inflation expectations for the next five years climbed to 3.5%, the highest since 1995.

"There are many irons in the fire, and markets don’t have the privilege of looking much beyond daily developments," said Francesco Pesole, currency strategist at ING.

The dollar index , which tracks the currency against six peers, was very slightly lower at 106.48.

The U.S. currency rose 0.17% against the yen to 149.54, after sliding last week on the back of rising expectations of further rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

In commodity markets, gold remained well supported at $2,946 an ounce , having climbed for eight weeks in a row.

Oil has been heading in the other direction in part on speculation an eventual peace deal on Ukraine could see sanctions eased on Russia, boosting its oil exports.

Brent was flat at $74.37 a barrel, continuing to trade around its lowest level since late December.

White Pearl Technology Group AB: Correction - Acquisition of Lumin4ry AB
data.description:
White Pearl Technology Group AB

INSIDE INFORMATION - CORRECTION

A previous version of this press release published on 24 February 2025 at 10:00 CET incorrectly referred to the acquired company as "Lumi4ry AB". The correct company name is "Lumin4ry AB". Below is the complete corrected press release.

White Pearl Technology Group AB (WPTG) has today entered into a binding agreement to acquire 100% of the shares in Lumin4ry AB ("Lumin4ry"), a Swedish IT services provider. Lumin4ry, which has a workforce of 100 people based in Stockholm, is expected to contribute with an annual revenue of approximately SEK 140 million and a profit of SEK 5 million. The purchase price amounts to SEK 9 million, paid in cash and a performance-based earn-out structure over 4 years, with potential additional consideration of up to SEK 11 million in WPTG shares. The acquisition strengthens WPTG's presence in the Nordic market and creates significant synergy opportunities.

Transaction Highlights:

  • Purchase price: Initial cash payment of SEK 9 million
  • Performance-based earn-out structure over 4 years, with potential additional consideration of up to SEK 11 million in WPTG shares

The acquisition will be financed through existing cash resources and newly issued WPTG shares for the earn-out component. The earn-out structure is divided into four periods:

  • Year 1: Up to SEK 2.75M in shares
  • Year 2: Up to SEK 2.75M in shares
  • Year 3: Up to SEK 2.75M in shares
  • Year 4: Up to SEK 2.75M in shares

Strategic Rationale:

  • Strengthens WPTG's direct presence in the Swedish market
  • Adds complementary IT service capabilities
  • Creates cross-selling opportunities in the Nordic region
  • Provides access to skilled IT professionals
  • Expected to be earnings accretive from year one

Marco Marangoni, CEO of White Pearl Technology Group, commented: "The acquisition of Lumin4ry marks an important step in our Nordic growth strategy. Their strong client relationships and technical expertise complement our existing operations and will help accelerate our expansion in Sweden."

Tony Lynden, CEO of Lumin4ry, added: "Joining WPTG provides an excellent platform for our continued growth. We look forward to leveraging WPTG's international presence and broader service offering to benefit our clients."

This information is such that White Pearl Technology Group AB is obliged to make it public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication through the agency of the contact person set out below at 10:30 CET on 21 February 2025.

For further information, please contact: Investor Relations White Pearl Technology Group AB Email: ir@whitepearltech.com Phone: +46 707 340804

The company's Certified Adviser is Amudova AB, email: info@amudova.se, phone: +46 8 546 017 58.

About White Pearl Technology Group AB:

White Pearl Technology Group AB (WPTG) is a global technology company specialising in digital transformation solutions. With a presence in over 30 countries and a team of more than 650 experts, WPTG helps organisations navigate the complexities of the digital age, offering services ranging from ICT and system integration to business software and digital innovation.

data.title: Malaysian Shares Start Week in Red Mirroring Regional Sentiment; Petronas Chemicals Slumps 10%
data.description:
Malaysian shares pared gains of the previous session to start the week in red, in line with broader regional sentiment.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, shed 6.78 points to end 0.43% lower at 1,584.25. The day range was between 1,577.95 and 1,586.39.

In corporate news, shares of Petronas Chemicals plunged about 10% on close, even as the company reported a profit attributable to owners of 519 million ringgit in the fourth quarter of 2024, surging from 112 million ringgit a year earlier.

Shares of Mah Sing Group dropped over 2% after it was served with a legal notice from One2Print over a 61,770 ringgit trespass judgment. The company also has to pay a 5% annual interest and 21,000 ringgit in costs.

Whereas, shares of Silver Ridge gained about 4% after it set the issue price of the ninth tranche of its private placement shares at 0.45 ringgit apiece. The management services provider proposed to issue up to 30% of the total number of its issued shares to private investors.

data.title: Singapore Shares Edge Lower in Likely Position Adjustments — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.1% lower at 3927.75 amid possible position adjustments by investors. The market could be digesting the government's recent measures to boost the country's equity market. The measures represent a step in the right direction, potentially setting off a positive cycle of attracting more investors and stimulating new listings, says IG market strategist Yeap Jun Rong in an email. Among decliners, Yangzijiang Shipbuilding fell 7.1%, SATS was down 3.3% and Seatrium lost 2.1%. Meanwhile, UOL Group rose 5.9% and Singapore Exchange climbed 3.9%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Serbia's Sloga to delist from Belgrade bourse
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), February 24 (SeeNews) - Serbia's agricultural producer Sloga [BEL:SLPP] said it plans to delist its shares from the Belgrade stock exchange after majority owner Tularis Trade squeezed out minority shareholders.

Sloga shareholders will vote on the delisting at an extraordinary general assembly scheduled for March 14, the company said on Friday in a filing with the Belgrade bourse.

In November, Tularis Trade, a Novi Sad-based wholesaler, increased its stake in Sloga to 91.02% from 86.95% following a buyout bid.

Sloga shareholders subsequently approved Tularis Trade's plans to squeeze out its minority shareholders, and Tularis Trade now owns 100% of Sloga, according to data from Serbia's central securities depository and clearing house.

Shares of Novi Sad-based Sloga, whose equity capital is divided into 246,101 shares, last traded on the Belgrade bourse on July 19, 2023, closing at 840 dinars ($7.5/ 7.2 euro).

Founded in 2010, Tularis Trade is fully owned by Serbian entrepreneur Zvonko Gobeljic, according to data from Serbia's business registers agency.

(1 euro = 117.180 dinars)

data.title: Barclays stays 'overweight' on E On, RWE after German elections
data.description:
** Barclays reiterates its "overweight" rating on German utilities firms E On and RWE after the German elections outcome

** The conservative CDU/CSU bloc came in first place on 28.5%, ahead of the far-right Alternative for Germany on 20.8%, according to provisional results

** "In addition to a potentially more business friendly platform, we believe investors would likely expect a Conservative-led government to pursue a more utility-friendly energy policy," Barclays writes in a note

** "The CDU/CSU proposals (network tariffs partly covered

by the government, requirement for favourable German network returns, expanding network regulator's role) appear to be constructive for network companies like E.ON and could support

our overall positive investment case" - Barclays

** The analysts see RWE as an even bigger winner after the elections, as the next government could support newly built flexible power plants

** E On and RWE shares both gain around 3%

data.title: Montenegro’s Hipotekarna Banka 2024 net profit jumps 50%
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), February 24 (SeeNews) - Montenegro’s Hipotekarna Banka [MNG:HIBP] said its net profit rose to 18.5 million euro ($19.4 million) last year from 12.3 million euro in 2023.

Hipotekarna Banka's net interest income increased to 30.8 million euro in 2024 from 23.1 million euro a year earlier, while net fee and commission income climbed to 13.6 million euro from 11.7 million euro, the lender said in a financial statement filed with the Montenegro Stock Exchange on Friday.

The bank's total assets rose to 1.09 billion euro at end-December from 921 million euro a year earlier.

According to latest central bank figures, Hipotekarna Banka was the second largest bank in terms of assets among 11 banks active in Montenegro at the end of September. It followed Crnogorska Komercijalna Banka (CKB), which is part of Hungarian banking group OTP. Hipotekarna also recorded the third largest profit in the local banking sector for the January-September period, following CKB and NLB Banka Podgorica, the local unit of Slovenia's Nova Ljubljanska Banka (NLB).

In December, financial holding company Agri Europe Cyprus (AEC) said it agreed to acquire 75% of Hipotekarna Banka for 75 million euro ($79 million), or 9.77 euro per share. The closure of the transaction is yet to be announced.

Hipotekarna Banka's shares closed flat at 9.3 euro on the Montenegro Stock Exchange on Friday.

Bourse data showed on Monday that the single largest ownership stake of 31.4% of Hipotekarna is held through a custodian account at the bank. Two Italian citizens, Paolo Gorgoni and Mario Gorgoni, follow, with individual stakes of 14.2% and 13.5%, respectively. Next are four members of Italy's Montinary family, controlling stakes of 4.9% each, while the remainder belongs to smaller shareholders.

($ = 0.956 euro)

data.title: Oil Prices Fall on Trade Concerns, Push for Russia-Ukraine Peace Deal — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices slide, with Brent crude and WTI both down 0.1% at $74.01 a barrel and $70.35 a barrel, respectively. Oil is under increasing downward pressure as sentiment sours, ING analysts say in a note. Friday saw a sharp selloff as investors cut positions in Brent, with trade and tariff concerns, alongside a push for a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine weighing on the market, ING analysts say. There is also intensifying speculation about OPEC+ production. The group is currently cutting supply by 2.2 million barrels a day and is scheduled to hike output from April, though there are suggestions OPEC+ is considering a delay, ING says. Any such delay would lead to a change in oil balance, leaving the market relatively tighter than expected, ING adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)
